gauge.name: Fox River at Algonquin tailwater

impacts.statement: Inundation of the floodplain reaches more than 1 block from the river in Algonquin.
Main Street is threatened in Algonquin.
Numerous structures are threatened along Main Street in Algonquin. North Water Reclamation Facility property in Elgin is threatened.
Algonquin Road (IL-62) is threatened west of the river in Algonquin.

Inundation reaches up to 2 blocks from the river in Willow Lake Estates in Elgin. Railroad tracks are threatened in downtown Elgin. Multiple structures threatened in downtown Carpentersville. Duncan Avenue threatened north of Elgin.
Widespread inundation of roadways and structures reaches 2 blocks from the river near Freeman Park north of downtown East Dundee. A few structures threatened along 1st Street in West Dundee.

Residences threatened along Riverview Drive and Holiday Drive in Holiday Hills. Residences threatened along Riverside Drive West south of McHenry. Structures threatened along North Harrison Street in Algonquin. A few structures threatened along Washington Street in Carpentersville. Algonquin Wastewater Treatment Facility property threatened. Main Street threatened in Carpentersville.

Backwater causes Crystal Creek to threaten structures near the village hall in Algonquin. Multiple structures threatened along Wenholz Avenue, Michigan Avenue, North Street, Barrington Avenue, and 2nd Street in East Dundee.

A few structures threatened along Water Street in East Dundee. A few structures threatened along 2nd Street in West Dundee. Structures along Riverside Drive and Grove Ave are threatened in Elgin. Backwater causes Crystal Creek to threaten low-lying sections of Towne Park in Algonquin.

Residences threatened along Edwards Avenue in East Dundee. Prairie Trail is threatened at the river in Algonquin. Parking lots threatened in downtown Carpentersville. Low-lying parking lots threatened at Judson College in Elgin.

Structures threatened along River Street, Johnson Street, and Maiden Lane in East Dundee. North Harrison Street threatened near Riverside Park in Algonquin. Washington Street threatened in Carpentersville. Water Street, Edwards Avenue, and Elgin Avenue threatened in East Dundee. Bayview Road threatened in Elgin. Low-lying areas of Festival Park are threatened in Elgin.

Residences threatened along Oceola Drive in Algonquin. A few residences threatened along McBride Street in Elgin. Jayne Street and Beach Drive threatened in Algonquin. River Street threatened in East Dundee. Jerusha Avenue threatened in Elgin. Fox River Trail threatened between East Dundee and Elgin.

Structures threatened near Elgin Avenue and Corey Street south of East Dundee. Lincoln Avenue threatened in West Dundee. Filip Street and Fox River Drive threatened in Algonquin. Fox River Trail threatened south of downtown Elgin.

Structures threatened along Fox River Drive in East Dundee. Parking lots threatened in downtown West Dundee. Low-lying sections of Cornish Park are inundated in Algonquin. Low-lying sections of Trout Park inundated north of Elgin.
Winaki Trail and Onaway Trail are threatened south of Algonquin. Athletic fields inundated at Judson College in Elgin.
Fox River Drive threatened in East Dundee. Boathouses threatened along the river in Cary.
Water overflows low-lying river banks along Grove Street in Valley View, along River Shore Drive in Cary, and along Riverview Drive and Holiday Drive in Holiday Hills.
Low-lying trails threatened at Walton Island Park in Elgin.

impactsLowWaters.impact: Low water levels begin to impact operations at Stratton and Algonquin Dams.
Approximate stage of the 1-in-10 annual chance flow (7-day average).

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past and future precipitation.  The  stages/flows that will occur may be different if future rainfall is different than forecast.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
